title: Dispatch model-resolution divergence — bundled specs are model-less by design and both dispatch sites hard-throw, with no model-config fallback
canonical_vocabulary: model-config precedence on the dispatch path
description: Zero of the 26 bundled specs declare model: — by design ('model comes from .project/model-config.json, not agent spec', pi-workflows/src/agent-spec.test.ts:291). The workflow executor resolves models as step.model ?? agentSpec.model ?? modelConfig.by_role[role] ?? modelConfig.default and OMITS --models when nothing resolves so pi's own default applies (pi-workflows/src/dispatch.ts:76-85; modelConfig read from the substrate model-config block, workflow-executor.ts:765-772). The dispatch path has none of this: call-agent-tool.ts:83-86 and work-order-loop.ts:174-177 hard-throw on a model-less spec, and pi-jit-agents has no fallback (compile.ts:574 passes spec.model verbatim). Class: even after the template class (FGAP-129) is fixed, every model-less spec — the entire bundled set — remains undispatchable on both dispatch paths.

evidence.reference: Class B: root cause, shape, the production precedence facts, per-anchor re-verified evidence

model resolution now compiled.model ?? spec.model ?? resolveDispatchModel(cwd, spec) with the residual throw naming the model-config block (TASK-104 fix in flight; previously an unconditional hard-throw on any model-less spec — the defect this gap tracks; work-order-loop.ts resolves identically and omits the subprocess --model when null)
:76-85 the proven precedence incl. --models omission when nothing resolves; modelConfig sourced from the model-config block (workflow-executor.ts:765-772)

impact: Bundled-agent dispatch fails 100% of the time on a model axis independent of template resolution; the dispatch surface contradicts the spec-authoring convention that models live in model-config, forcing spec forks or hand-edits.
proposed_resolution: Per the derived dispatch model-policy decision (user-approved plan, 2026-07-08): a resolveDispatchModel(cwd, spec) helper implementing spec.model ?? model-config.by_role[spec.role] ?? model-config.default (absent block = null fall-through, mirroring the executor); work-order subprocess dispatch omits --model when null (pi defaults inside the subprocess); in-process call-agent keeps the throw when nothing resolves, with the error naming the model-config block as the remedy; precedence unit cells.
